title: Strings, Byte Buffers, Length, Capacity, and Encoding · Stakeholder Native Systems Mission
summary: A rural air-quality network. The network needs a portable record buffer and text boundary with checked dimensions, stable binary encoding, explicit length and capacity, accessible diagnostics, and changed device profiles. Planned artifact: Deliver Bounded Environmental Sensor Buffer with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject overflowing addition, multiplication, alignment, and growth calculations before allocation using portable checked patterns or C23 checked arithmetic.
State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.
Encode and decode external integers byte-by-byte or with defined conversion APIs instead of dumping native objects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Checked Size Arithmetic
steps.instruction:
project case — A project native-systems team handles memory case 4355. Produce bounded C evidence to preserve length, capacity, and termination invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Checked Size Arithmetic.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for reject overflowing addition, multiplication, alignment, and growth calculations before allocation using portable checked patterns or c23 checked arithmetic.
